content: We can reframe the ICM curiosity mechanism as a built-in function caller for tool-use autonomous agents, where the intrinsic reward for exploring novel states directly triggers targeted tool search and invocation instead of passive environmental exploration. This turns undirected intrinsic curiosity into a goal-oriented tool discovery process that lets agents expand their own toolkits beyond the pre-defined set given by developers.
source_topics: tool use function calling autonomous agent
trigger_topic: tool use function calling autonomous agent
